problem: Suppose 5 different integers are randomly chosen from between 20 and 69, inclusive.  What is the probability that they each have a different tens digit?

classified_questions.question: Can you restate the problem in your own words?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What are the five possible tens digits of the chosen integers?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What is the total number of ways to choose 5 integers without any restrictions?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Why must there be exactly one integer with each tens digit if they all have different tens digits?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many options are there for each tens digit to choose from?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the probability that 5 integers drawn all have different tens digits?
classified_questions.label: b
